question_structure.context: During the Spring Festival holiday, national highways are toll-free, and cars can pass through toll stations without stopping. However, cars are required to maintain a speed no greater than $v_{0} = 6 \, \mathrm{m/s}$ when passing through the $x_{0} = 9 \, \mathbf{m}$ interval in front of the toll station window. Currently, two cars, Car A and Car B, are traveling at constant speeds of $v_{A} = 20 \, \mathrm{m/s}$ and $v_{B} = 34 \, \mathrm{m/s}$ respectively on the straight road in front of the toll station. Car A is ahead of Car B. The driver of Car A notices the toll station ahead and begins to decelerate uniformly with an acceleration of $a_{A} = 2 \, \mathbf{m/s}^{2}$.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The driver of car A needs to start braking at least how far away from the toll booth window to avoid violating regulations.
question_structure.sub_question_2: If the driver of car A brakes and reaches a speed of exactly $6\mathrm{m/s}$ at a point $9\mathrm{m}$ before the toll booth window and continues to pass the toll booth window at this constant speed, and the driver of car B, upon noticing car A braking, starts to brake with a deceleration of $a_{B}=4\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$ after a reaction time of $t_{0}=0.5\mathrm{s}$. To avoid a collision between the two cars and ensure that car B does not exceed the speed limit in the $9\mathrm{m}$ zone before the toll booth window, what is the minimum distance between car A and car B when the driver of car A starts to brake?

sub_question_1.step_1: The displacement of car A as its speed decreases from $20\mathrm{m/s}$ to $6\mathrm{m/s}$ is: $x_{1}={\frac{v_{A}^{2}-v_{0}^{2}}{2a_{A}}{=}{\frac{400-36}{2\times2}}\mathrm{m}=91\mathrm{m}$
sub_question_1.step_2: The distance between the two cars is $x_{2}=x_{1}+x_{0}=100\mathrm{m}$, which means the cars need to start braking at least $100\mathrm{m}$ away from the toll booth window.

sub_question_2.step_3: Let the time when the speeds of the two cars are the same be $t$. From the kinematic equation, we have: $v_{B}a_{B}(t-t_{0})=v_{A}-a_{A}t$. Substituting the data, we find: $t=8s$.
sub_question_2.step_4: The speed when the two vehicles have the same velocity is $v = 4\,\mathrm{m/s}$. If the two vehicles do not collide when their speeds are reduced to $4\,\mathrm{m/s}$, then they certainly will not collide when their speeds are reduced to $6\,\mathrm{m/s}$.
sub_question_2.step_5: The minimum distance between the two is calculated when both decelerate to $6\mathrm{m/s}$. The displacement of car B from the initial speed of $34\mathrm{m/s}$ to $6\mathrm{m/s}$ is: $x_{3}=v_{B}t_{0}+\frac{v_{B}^{2}-v_{0}^{2}}{2a_{B}}$. Substituting the data, we get: $x_{3}=157m$.
sub_question_2.step_6: The time taken by car B is $t_{1}=t_{0}+\frac{v_{B}-v_{0}}{a_{B}}=7.5s$.
sub_question_2.step_7: The time required for the two cars to reach a speed of $6\mathrm{m/s}$ is $t_{2}=\frac{v_{A}-v_{0}}{a_{A}}=7s$.
sub_question_2.step_8: At this moment, the displacement of car A: $x_{1}^{\prime} = x_{1}+v_{0}\Delta t=91m$
sub_question_2.step_9: The distance between the two cars is $x_{3} - x_{1}^{\prime} = 66 \, \text{m}$.

step_1.physical_theorem: Kinematic equation for uniformly accelerated motion

result_quantity.name: Displacement of car A while decelerating from 20 m/s to 6 m/s
Minimum distance from the toll booth for car A to start braking

step_3.physical_theorem: Kinematic equation for uniformly accelerated motion

result_quantity.name: Time when the speeds of the two cars are the same
Speed when two cars have same velocity

step_5.physical_theorem: Kinematic equation for uniformly accelerated motion

result_quantity.name: Displacement of car B while decelerating from 34 m/s to 6 m/s

step_6.physical_theorem: Kinematic equation for uniformly accelerated motion

result_quantity.name: Time taken by car B to decelerate from 34 m/s to 6 m/s

step_7.physical_theorem: Kinematic equation for uniformly accelerated motion

result_quantity.name: Time taken by car A to decelerate from 20 m/s to 6 m/s

step_8.physical_theorem: Displacement Calculation for constant speed and uniformly accelerated motion

result_quantity.name: Displacement of car A when car A reaches 6m/s
Minimum distance between car A and car B when car A starts to brake
